mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.2% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.2%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing, causing negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive or flat when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate compression that erodes equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates alongside stable rates, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains wage income and consumption velocity, providing a structural floor for corporate revenues and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns over consecutive quarters, or the signal IC drops below zero in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below 0.5% annualized growth triggers immediate risk-off positioning as investors price in recessionary tail risks, depressing short-term market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in >70% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase signals productive capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of recovery periods, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.
